nodes.summary: Derives the two-axis coverage receipt ledger — Axis A coverage (source to deliverable) and Axis B containment (deliverable to source) — from joins that already exist at the dispatch site, with deterministic locator-anchor resolution and figure-facet rows.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 models with extra="forbid" and validate_assignment per the project schema checklist; the two axes are joined per row but never merged into one status.

nodes.name: AnchorResolution
nodes.summary: Result of the deterministic locator-anchor resolution for a source point (anchor kind, matched slide key, confidence).

nodes.name: CoverageReceiptRow
nodes.summary: One receipt row joining a source point's coverage status (Axis A) and containment status (Axis B), kept as orthogonal fields.

nodes.name: CoverageReceipt
nodes.summary: The derived coverage-receipt.json artifact: full per-point accountability ledger with plan-view projections and shippability helpers.

nodes.name: _figure_token_set
nodes.summary: Extracts a normalized figure-token set from text for the figure-facet containment comparison.

nodes.name: _derive_must_cover
nodes.summary: Derives the must-cover flag for a source point from its coverage intents.

nodes.name: _derive_row
nodes.summary: Derives a full receipt row for one source point: resolves the deterministic anchor first, then projects coverage and containment statuses from existing joins.

nodes.name: _derive_figure_facet_row
nodes.summary: Derives the figure-facet sub-row verifying that source figures are actually carried into deck and narration surfaces.

nodes.name: _derive_containment
nodes.summary: Derives the Axis B containment status (verbatim_preserved/altered/risky) by comparing deliverable text back to the source span.

nodes.name: _resolve_anchor_for_point
nodes.summary: Resolves the deterministic locator anchor for a source point; no anchor forces status missing, regardless of any LLM judgment.

nodes.name: derive_coverage_receipt
nodes.summary: Top-level idempotent derivation of the CoverageReceipt from coverage annotations, anchors, and marshalled surfaces.

nodes.name: coverage_plan_view
nodes.summary: Projects the receipt into the operator-facing plan view grouped by slide.

nodes.name: coverage_plan_view_from_dicts
nodes.summary: Plan-view projection accepting raw dict rows loaded from a disk receipt.

nodes.name: g0_enrichment.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models for the frozen, operator-confirmable G0 pre-pass result: the typed source manifest, candidate provisional learning objectives, enumeration provenance and traversal roots, plus the anti-anchoring artifacts (independent-parse sidecar and dissent ledger).
nodes.languageNotes: Follows the repo Pydantic-v2 checklist: ConfigDict(extra="forbid", validate_assignment=True) and timezone-aware datetimes; adequacy is deliberately absent (every provisional LO carries adequacy=None).

nodes.name: IndependentParse
nodes.summary: A4 anti-anchoring sidecar: Marcus's independent parse recorded before seeing the specialist output.

nodes.name: OperatorMerge
nodes.summary: Record of the operator's merge decision over the independent parse versus the specialist output.

nodes.name: Dissent
nodes.summary: A3 dissent-ledger entry capturing disagreement between parses with a resolution status.

nodes.name: ReconcileView
nodes.summary: Reconciliation view joining independent parse, specialist output, and dissents for operator confirmation at gate G0E.

nodes.name: CitationResolution
nodes.summary: Resolution record binding an extracted citation to a resolvable source location and resolution status.

nodes.name: G0EnrichmentResult
nodes.summary: The frozen result artifact of the G0 pre-pass: typed source manifest, provisional LOs, enumeration provenance, and anti-anchoring artifacts.

nodes.name: assert_run_dissent_invariant
nodes.summary: Asserts the run-level dissent invariant so unresolved dissents block operator confirmation.

nodes.name: corpus_fingerprint
nodes.summary: Computes the deterministic corpus fingerprint used as the cache and replay key for the frozen enrichment result.

nodes.name: irene_refinement.py
nodes.summary: Irene LO-refinement artifacts plus the signed LO-delta contract: refines provisional learning objectives in place on the same objective_id, attaches advisory source-adequacy alerts, and enforces count reconciliation with no silent drops for operator ratify-gate G0R.

nodes.name: LODeltaEntry
nodes.summary: One signed LO-delta ledger entry recording a per-objective change channel (refined text, Bloom level, adequacy) with digests.

nodes.name: LODeltaLedger
nodes.summary: The signed LO-delta contract: a reconcilable ledger of all refinement channels with count reconciliation and no silent drops.

nodes.name: IreneRefinementResult
nodes.summary: Frozen result artifact of Irene's LO refinement pass, re-presented to the operator at ratify-gate G0R.

nodes.name: _deterministic_adequacy
nodes.summary: Deterministic offline heuristic assigning an advisory SourceAdequacy alert for a learning objective.

nodes.name: refine_one
nodes.summary: Refines a single provisional LO in place (same objective_id) via the advance_lo transition guard with actor irene.

nodes.name: build_refinement_result
nodes.summary: Builds the full refinement result and delta ledger over all confirmed provisional LOs.

nodes.name: _live_refine
nodes.summary: Live-LLM refinement path producing refined LO statements and Bloom levels.

nodes.name: _parse_live_refinement
nodes.summary: Parses and validates the live refinement response into typed deltas, failing loud on malformed payloads.

nodes.name: assert_completeness
nodes.summary: Asserts count reconciliation across all ledger channels so every input LO is accounted for with no silent drops.

nodes.name: ratify_refined_los
nodes.summary: Applies the operator's G0R verdict, advancing refined LOs to ratified — the operator, never the model, advances status.

nodes.name: learning_objective.py
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for the canonical provenance-anchored LearningObjective entity with a lifecycle transition guard (provisional to refined to ratified), replacing three legacy in-code LO representations via adapter bridges.
nodes.languageNotes: Immutable objective_id carried across the whole lifecycle keys the learning_objective_map DB table; transitions are only legal through the advance_lo guard.

nodes.name: SourceRef
nodes.summary: Resolvable provenance reference binding a learning objective to its source component locations.

nodes.name: SourceAdequacy
nodes.summary: Advisory per-LO source-adequacy alert — never blocks the pipeline, forces a drop, or gates a status transition.

nodes.name: LearningObjective
nodes.summary: Canonical lifecycle-aware learning objective entity with immutable objective_id, provenance refs, and Bloom level.

nodes.name: advance_lo
nodes.summary: The single transition guard for LO status changes; raises IllegalTransition on out-of-order or wrong-actor transitions.

nodes.name: pedagogy_annotation.py
nodes.summary: P3 pedagogical annotation overlay: one additive, never-gating PedagogyAnnotation per annotatable typed component carrying Bloom level, pedagogical role, deterministic teaches_after ordering, a teachable verdict derived from front-matter status, and a bounded rationale — with both offline and live-LLM passes.

nodes.name: PedagogyAnnotation
nodes.summary: The P3 overlay model per annotatable component: Bloom level, pedagogical role, teaches_after ordering, teachable verdict, and bounded audit rationale.

nodes.name: is_annotatable
nodes.summary: Predicate deciding whether a typed component type receives a pedagogy annotation.

nodes.name: compute_teaches_after
nodes.summary: Computes the deterministic teaches_after ordering from document ordinals.

nodes.name: derive_teachable
nodes.summary: Derives the teachable verdict from the front-matter resolution_status, never from citation_resolutions.

nodes.name: assert_p2_frontmatter_present
nodes.summary: Asserts each component carries the required P2 universal-md front-matter fields before annotation.

nodes.name: assert_pedagogy_referential_invariant
nodes.summary: Asserts every annotation references an existing component and that its LO ids resolve.

nodes.name: assert_pedagogy_teachable_consistency
nodes.summary: Asserts teachable verdicts remain consistent with component resolution status.

nodes.name: _offline_pedagogy_pass
nodes.summary: Deterministic offline annotation pass used when live dispatch is off.

nodes.name: _extract_pedagogy_rows
nodes.summary: Extracts candidate annotation rows from the enrichment card payload.

nodes.name: _build_live_annotations
nodes.summary: Builds validated PedagogyAnnotation objects from parsed live-LLM rows with enum normalization and deterministic fallbacks.

nodes.name: _live_pedagogy_pass
nodes.summary: Live-LLM annotation pass: renders the prompt, dispatches the call, and parses the response.

nodes.name: _render_live_pedagogy_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders the live pedagogy prompt embedding component front matter and the closed enums.

nodes.name: build_pedagogy_annotations
nodes.summary: Entry point choosing the offline or live pass and returning the validated annotation set.

nodes.name: planning_context.py
nodes.summary: Loads optional planning context (purpose, audience, learning-objective briefs, assessment) by merging on-disk planning-ratification.json and ratified-los.json under a run directory, and assesses LO coverage of plan units; absent artifacts yield None so default Irene behavior is preserved.

nodes.name: PlanningContext
nodes.summary: Optional planning frame (purpose, audience, LO briefs, assessment) merged from ratification artifacts for Irene Pass-1 emphasis.

nodes.name: _los_from_ratified
nodes.summary: Loads learning-objective briefs from the on-disk ratified-los.json artifact.

nodes.name: _from_ratification
nodes.summary: Builds a PlanningContext from planning-ratification.json content.

nodes.name: _significant_tokens
nodes.summary: Tokenizes text into significant, stopword-filtered tokens for LO-coverage matching.

nodes.name: assess_lo_coverage
nodes.summary: Assesses how well plan units cover each ratified LO via significant-token overlap scoring, producing a coverage receipt.

nodes.name: assert_lo_coverage_or_fail
nodes.summary: Raises PlanningContextError when LO coverage falls below the acceptance threshold.

nodes.name: load_planning_context
nodes.summary: Loads the optional planning context from a run directory; absent artifacts return None.

nodes.name: planning_ratification.py
nodes.summary: Lightweight planning ratification recorder for the Phase-2 selection bridge: captures purpose, audience, plan choices, and gap-fill tradeoffs, then emits a canonical RatifiedLessonPlanCollateralIntent onto the existing selection edge.

nodes.name: GapFillTradeoff
nodes.summary: Records one gap-fill tradeoff decision: the gap, the chosen approach, and its rationale.

nodes.name: PlanningRatificationRecord
nodes.summary: Canonical record of the operator's planning ratification: purpose, audience, plan choices, tradeoffs, and workflow.

nodes.name: SelectionDelta
nodes.summary: Delta between the baseline and the ratified component selections.

nodes.name: _intent_for_workflow
nodes.summary: Maps the ratified workflow to the canonical S8 RatifiedLessonPlanCollateralIntent.

nodes.name: _assert_gap_fill_consistent_with_workflow
nodes.summary: Validates that gap-fill tradeoffs are consistent with the chosen workflow.

nodes.name: ratify_planning_decision
nodes.summary: Builds and validates the ratification record and derives the collateral intent for the selection edge.

nodes.name: write_ratification_artifacts
nodes.summary: Writes planning-ratification.json and its companion view into the run directory.

nodes.name: compute_selection_delta
nodes.summary: Computes the SelectionDelta between the baseline and ratified selections.

nodes.name: run_asset_index.py
nodes.summary: UDAC v1 Run Asset Index (RAI): the neutral access-and-use contract with canonical digesting, typed legacy modes, fail-loud loaders, and asset resolution to a read-only view — placed models-side so both the orchestrator (builder) and specialists (consumers) can import it without violating the M3 layering contract.
nodes.languageNotes: RESOLVE-ASSET-LIVES-NEUTRAL discipline: this module imports no app.marcus.orchestrator module so specialists can consume the resolved view without a layering violation.

nodes.name: RunAssetEntry
nodes.summary: One RAI entry: asset id, path, digest, authority status, and declared usage.

nodes.name: ResolvedAsset
nodes.summary: Read-only resolved view of an asset handed to consuming specialists.

nodes.name: RunAssetIndex
nodes.summary: The Run Asset Index built by the orchestrator at ratification gates and consumed by specialists as the single access contract.

nodes.name: recompute_digest_from_disk
nodes.summary: Recomputes an asset digest from disk, honoring the typed legacy mode and digest algorithm.

nodes.name: compare_digests
nodes.summary: Compares recorded versus recomputed digests, classifying match, mismatch, and legacy outcomes.

nodes.name: resolve_asset
nodes.summary: Neutral asset resolution: validates authority and digest and returns a ResolvedAsset; raises AssetResolutionError on any contract breach.

nodes.name: load_rai
nodes.summary: Fail-loud loader for the Run Asset Index from a run directory.

nodes.name: write_rai
nodes.summary: Writes the canonical RAI JSON artifact (orchestrator-side writer, separated from loaders).

nodes.name: source_assessment.py
nodes.summary: Assesses available source material for lesson-planning ratification as a thin adapter over course-source input bundles and curated corpus directory leaves; performs no remote ingestion and fills no missing media.

nodes.name: SourceAssessment
nodes.summary: Assessment summary of available source material (kinds, counts, gap summary) for planning ratification.

nodes.name: assess_from_input_bundle
nodes.summary: Builds a SourceAssessment from a lesson-planning input bundle.

nodes.name: assess_from_corpus_dir
nodes.summary: Builds a SourceAssessment by scanning a curated corpus directory on disk.

nodes.name: assessments_differ_in_kind_or_count
nodes.summary: Detects material differences between two assessments (source kind or count changes).

nodes.name: source_point.py
nodes.summary: SourcePoint model — the atomic teaching-assertion unit of the coverage axis, produced by re-segmenting a slide's presentation-note block; a child sub-locator of an existing TypedComponent whose only sanctioned join surface is the parent component_id.

nodes.name: SourcePoint
nodes.summary: Atomic teaching-assertion unit of the coverage axis with verbatim span, assertion kind, and coverage intents; id is component_id#ordinal.

nodes.name: verbatim_required_for
nodes.summary: Policy predicate deciding whether a point's assertion kind requires verbatim preservation.

nodes.name: make_source_point_id
nodes.summary: Constructs the child id component_id#ordinal — a sub-locator, never a join key.

nodes.name: join_key
nodes.summary: The only sanctioned join surface: returns the parent component_id for all downstream joins.

nodes.name: assert_join_key
nodes.summary: Asserts a join uses the parent component id, rejecting #ordinal child ids as join keys.

nodes.name: derive_coverage_intents
nodes.summary: Derives per-point coverage intents (must_cover, verbatim) from the assertion kind and note content.

nodes.name: source_type.py
nodes.summary: Closed 10-type source taxonomy (plus an other:<label> escape hatch) with TypedSource and TypedComponent models for the G0 enrichment brick; TYPE is recorded independently of the operator directive role and the two are never overloaded onto one field.

nodes.name: OtherSourceType
nodes.summary: Escape-hatch type other:<label> for spans outside the closed 10-type taxonomy, surfaced flagged rather than consumed.

nodes.name: TypedSource
nodes.summary: A typed source-file record whose TYPE classification is independent of the operator directive role.

nodes.name: TypedComponent
nodes.summary: An intra-document typed component with document-hierarchy locator and verbatim excerpt.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry for the Marcus orchestrator (4A conversation loop, plan-lock commit, downstream fan-out): re-exports routing and supervisor surfaces and defines the NegotiatorSeam; external callers use the Marcus facade instead of importing this package directly.

nodes.name: NegotiatorSeam
nodes.summary: Seam object exposing the negotiation surface of the 4A conversation loop to the supervisor.

nodes.name: chooser_publisher.py
nodes.summary: Publishes the per-slide Storyboard-A A/B chooser page to GitHub Pages at gate G2B: renders the chooser HTML from Gary's per-slide PNGs and pushes a self-contained pack so the operator can pick variants slide-by-slide; the selection code returns as the G2B verdict.

nodes.name: _per_slide_options
nodes.summary: Collects the A/B variant options (per-slide PNGs and metadata) from Gary's dispatched outputs.

nodes.name: publish_chooser_for_gate
nodes.summary: Renders the per-slide A/B chooser HTML pack and publishes it to gh-pages for the G2B pick.

nodes.name: _git_publish_dir
nodes.summary: Delegates the clone-stage-commit-push transport to the shared gh-pages publish helper.

nodes.name: conversation_persistence.py
nodes.summary: Persists Marcus conversation turns with a tamper-evident SHA256 hash chain: canonical JSON digesting, chained turn writes, ordered loading, and full chain verification that fails loud on breaks.

nodes.name: canonical_json_bytes
nodes.summary: Serializes a payload to canonical sorted JSON bytes for stable digesting.

nodes.name: compute_turn_digest
nodes.summary: Computes the SHA256 chain digest of a turn over its canonical bytes plus the previous turn digest.

nodes.name: latest_turn_digest
nodes.summary: Returns the digest of the most recent persisted turn for chain continuation.

nodes.name: load_turn
nodes.summary: Loads a persisted conversation turn from disk.

nodes.name: write_turn
nodes.summary: Persists a conversation turn with its chained digest, decision card, and monotonic index ordering.

nodes.name: verify_chain
nodes.summary: Verifies the tamper-evident digest chain across all persisted turns, raising ConversationChainBrokenError on any break.

nodes.name: coverage_anchors.py
nodes.summary: Pure, offline-testable core for the deck side of the coverage join: reconciles final slide ids to source slide_keys from the slide_briefs source_ref lineage and projects deck and narration text anchors; drifted briefs are omitted, never guessed.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately replicates minimal loader logic instead of cross-importing app.specialists, keeping the M3 layering boundary clean (mirrors Irene's loader-replication discipline).

nodes.name: resolve_slide_key_map
nodes.summary: Builds the primary {final_slide_id: slide_key} map from the slide_briefs source_ref to plan_units lineage; drifted briefs are omitted, never guessed.

nodes.name: resolve_slide_key_lineage
nodes.summary: Resolves the full slide-key lineage, including clustered sub-slides that share one source key.

nodes.name: _union_text
nodes.summary: Unions a slide's deck text surfaces into a single anchor text.

nodes.name: build_coverage_anchors
nodes.summary: Pure reconciliation core projecting deck and narration text anchors keyed by slide_key for the coverage join.

nodes.name: coverage_gate_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrator wiring for the coverage gate (T6): env feature flag (default OFF), disk-primary receipt I/O, and the both-walks seam callable invoked at the shared dispatch site to enforce the gate before any audio-spending specialist runs.

nodes.name: coverage_gate_active
nodes.summary: Env-flag predicate for the coverage gate; default OFF keeps the existing pipeline byte-identical.

nodes.name: mark_coverage_receipt_expected
nodes.summary: Drops the receipt-expected marker first so a later derive failure still trips the fail-loud gate instead of silently bypassing it.

nodes.name: write_coverage_receipt
nodes.summary: Writes the derived coverage receipt to its disk-primary path in the run directory.

nodes.name: note_bearing_content_exists
nodes.summary: Checks whether the run's enrichment carries note-bearing content that makes the coverage gate applicable.

nodes.name: enforce_coverage_gate_before_audio
nodes.summary: Both-walks seam callable: loads the receipt from disk and asserts the coverage gate before audio-spending specialist dispatch.

nodes.name: coverage_runner.py
nodes.summary: Runner-integration glue for the G3 coverage seam: marshals the run's own deck and narration export surfaces into anchors, derives and writes the coverage receipt as a canonical RAI artifact, and renders the operator-facing coverage report HTML — via one shared helper called at both walk sites.

nodes.name: _load_coverage_annotations
nodes.summary: Loads the authored coverage annotations from the frozen g0-enrichment.json artifact.

nodes.name: _select_plan_units
nodes.summary: Selects the lesson-plan units in scope for the run's coverage join.

nodes.name: _collision_aware_slide_key_map
nodes.summary: Builds the slide-key map while detecting and disambiguating breadcrumb-ordinal collisions.

nodes.name: _marshal_coverage_surfaces
nodes.summary: Marshals the run's own deck rows and narration segments (run-dir export SSOT) into coverage anchors.

nodes.name: _write_coverage_report_html
nodes.summary: Renders and writes the operator-facing coverage report HTML via the storyboard emitter.

nodes.name: _derive_and_write_coverage_receipt
nodes.summary: The one shared G3 helper both walk sites call: drops the expected marker first, derives the receipt, writes the RAI artifact, and renders the report.

nodes.name: dispatch_adapter.py
nodes.summary: Production composition adapter for scaffolded specialists: composes specialist graphs behind the manifest-compiled run graph, resolving builders dynamically and marshalling run state, cache state, and production-envelope contributions across dispatch.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses LangGraph InMemorySaver checkpointing and importlib-based dynamic builder resolution against the manifest compiler's SPECIALIST_ALIASES.

nodes.name: ProductionDispatchAdapter
nodes.summary: Adapter that composes scaffolded specialist graphs behind the manifest-compiled production graph, marshalling run/cache state and returning envelope contributions.

nodes.name: enrichment_consumption.py
nodes.summary: P5-S2 orchestrator-side consumption layer projecting the frozen, operator-confirmed G0 enrichment card into the two learner-facing seams: per-slide role-derived voice-direction seeds for narration (Irene Pass-2) and a sanitized deck-enrichment hint for the deck composer.

nodes.name: deck_enrichment_active
nodes.summary: Env-flag predicate for deck-side enrichment consumption.

nodes.name: role_to_voice_direction
nodes.summary: Maps a pedagogical role to its frozen voice_direction default via the PEDAGOGICAL_ROLE_TO_VOICE table.

nodes.name: source_slide_ordinals
nodes.summary: Extracts the source slide ordinals a component teaches from its document locator.

nodes.name: project_role_derived_voice_by_slide
nodes.summary: Consumer A: projects role-derived voice-direction seeds keyed by slide ordinal for the narration specialist.

nodes.name: _candidate_narration_seeds_by_ordinal
nodes.summary: Collects candidate narration role seeds per slide ordinal, dropping ambiguous 0-or-many matches.

nodes.name: project_ambiguous_narration_ordinals
nodes.summary: Reports slide ordinals whose narration seed match was ambiguous, so drops are logged rather than silent.

nodes.name: project_deck_enrichment_hint
nodes.summary: Consumer B: projects a sanitized deck-enrichment hint statement for the deck composer.

nodes.name: g0_enrichment_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Runner-wired G0 pre-pass orchestration: enumerates the corpus, segments every source file into typed components with locators and verbatim excerpts, extracts provisional LOs offline or via a live-LLM pass, caches the frozen result keyed to a corpus fingerprint, and attaches pedagogy/coverage front matter for operator confirm-gate G0E.
nodes.languageNotes: The live path includes truncated-JSON salvage parsing so a cut-off LLM payload degrades to a recoverable parse error rather than silent data loss.

nodes.name: resolve_enrichment_mode
nodes.summary: Resolves the enrichment mode (off, offline, live) from environment flags and the run directive.

nodes.name: g0_enrichment_active
nodes.summary: Predicate for whether the G0 pre-pass runs at all.

nodes.name: _split_components
nodes.summary: Segments one source file's text into N typed components, each with a document-hierarchy locator and verbatim excerpt.

nodes.name: _file_components
nodes.summary: Produces the typed component set for a single enumerated corpus file.

nodes.name: flag_ungrounded_components
nodes.summary: Flags components whose excerpt is not grounded in the normalized source text — the anti-hallucination groundedness check.

nodes.name: _offline_pre_pass
nodes.summary: Deterministic offline pre-pass building the typed manifest and provisional LOs without any LLM.

nodes.name: _build_dissent
nodes.summary: Builds A3 dissent-ledger entries where the independent parse and the specialist output disagree.

nodes.name: _parse_live_extraction_response
nodes.summary: Parses the live extraction response, salvaging truncated JSON payloads and failing loud on unrecoverable ones.

nodes.name: _live_pre_pass
nodes.summary: Live-LLM pre-pass dispatching the extraction prompt over the corpus summary.

nodes.name: _parse_live_payload
nodes.summary: Validates and coerces live payload rows into typed components and provisional learning objectives.

nodes.name: build_enrichment_result
nodes.summary: Assembles the frozen G0EnrichmentResult (manifest, LOs, provenance, dissent) with corpus-fingerprint caching for replay determinism.

nodes.name: run_g0_enrichment
nodes.summary: Runner-wired entry point: enumerates the corpus, runs the pre-pass, attaches pedagogy and coverage front matter, and freezes the result to the run directory.

nodes.name: load_enrichment_result
nodes.summary: Loads the frozen g0-enrichment.json result from a run directory.

nodes.name: gate_runner.py
nodes.summary: Shared gate-runner guardrails from the Slab 7a closeout: calibration-tripwire evaluation over gate verdict history, engagement-decay reporting, and Marcus duality-boundary assertions keeping orchestrator and persona sides separated.

nodes.name: evaluate_calibration_tripwire
nodes.summary: Evaluates the calibration tripwire over gate verdict history, flagging drift beyond configured thresholds.

nodes.name: emit_engagement_decay_report
nodes.summary: Emits the engagement-decay JSONL report across gate interactions.

nodes.name: assert_marcus_duality_boundary
nodes.summary: Asserts the Marcus duality boundary — orchestrator-side code never crosses into persona-side surfaces.

nodes.name: assert_payload_duality_boundary
nodes.summary: Asserts payloads crossing the duality boundary carry no forbidden fields.

nodes.name: gh_pages_publish.py
nodes.summary: Shared gh-pages publish helper consolidating the clone-stage-commit-push transport with three fail-loud hygiene guards — retention pruning by git committer date, a published-size guard against the GitHub Pages 1 GB cap, and post-push build verification — so the public asset host can never silently freeze again.

nodes.name: _sanitize_pack
nodes.summary: Sanitizes a pack directory before publish: filename hygiene checks and token scrubbing.

nodes.name: prune_retention
nodes.summary: Deletes managed packs older than max_age_days by git committer date, honoring the protected allowlist and never touching the pack being published.

nodes.name: project_published_size
nodes.summary: Size guard: sums the checked-out working tree and raises SizeGuardRefusal before pushing past the Pages published-site cap.

nodes.name: _verify_page_live
nodes.summary: Polls the published URL until the new content is live or the timeout budget is exhausted.

nodes.name: verify_build_after_push
nodes.summary: Verifies the GitHub Pages build succeeded after push via the Pages build API.

nodes.name: git_publish_dir
nodes.summary: The consolidated clone, stage, commit, and push transport with the hygiene guards woven in.

nodes.name: load_hygiene_config
nodes.summary: Loads the retention, size-guard, and verify hygiene configuration for the publish helpers.

nodes.name: irene_refinement_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Runner-wired Irene refinement hook (Story G0-S3): consumes gate-#1-confirmed provisional LOs from the frozen enrichment artifact, refines each in place with Bloom level and advisory adequacy, freezes the signed LO-delta contract to irene-refinement.json, and feeds operator ratify-gate G0R.

nodes.name: irene_refinement_active
nodes.summary: Env-flag predicate for the Irene refinement brick.

nodes.name: _load_confirmed_provisional_los
nodes.summary: Reads the gate-#1-confirmed provisional LOs from the frozen g0-enrichment.json.

nodes.name: build_result_for_run
nodes.summary: Builds the refinement result for a run from its confirmed provisional LOs.

nodes.name: run_irene_refinement
nodes.summary: Runner-wired hook: refines confirmed LOs (offline or live), freezes the signed LO-delta contract to the run directory, and feeds gate G0R.

nodes.name: load_refinement_result
nodes.summary: Loads the frozen refinement result from a run directory.

nodes.name: load_refinement_full
nodes.summary: Loads the full refinement payload including the delta ledger and adequacy channels.

nodes.name: package_builders.py
nodes.summary: S3 pre-dispatch package builders: pure, deterministic transforms projecting upstream envelope contributions (ratified lesson plan plus creative directive) into the exact payload vocabulary a downstream specialist consumes — no LLM in the deterministic neck, invoked at their manifest node like pre_gate_marcus.

nodes.name: BuilderInputError
nodes.summary: Fail-loud error raised for missing or malformed upstream contributions at a builder node.

nodes.name: _unit_included
nodes.summary: Predicate for whether a plan unit is included in the dispatch scope.

nodes.name: build_gary_briefs
nodes.summary: Pure deterministic projection of the ratified lesson plan and creative directive into Gary's per-slide brief payload vocabulary.

nodes.name: _deck_enrichment_hint
nodes.summary: Fetches the sanitized deck-enrichment hint from the enrichment consumption layer.

nodes.name: run_builder_node
nodes.summary: Executes a builder at its manifest node and lands the output as a first-class envelope contribution keyed to that node.

nodes.name: picker_html_emitter.py
nodes.summary: Renders the static gh-pages styleguide picker page and decodes the SGP selection code the operator pastes back into marcus_spoc. Return-path twin of the chooser HTML emitter; roster loading and card CSS are imported from styleguide_picker so the two transports cannot drift, and the selection-code grammar is frozen in one place shared by the embedded JS encoder and the Python decoder.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds a full client-side JavaScript encoder as a Python string so the static page stays dependency-free and inert (write-locus stays on the marcus side).

nodes.name: build_selection_code
nodes.summary: Builds the canonical SGP-{run_tag}-A:{slug}[ B:{slug}] selection code string from a run tag and pick map, the reference implementation of the frozen grammar.

nodes.name: decode_picker_selection_code
nodes.summary: Parses and validates a pasted selection code against the expected run tag and SSOT slug membership, returning the {A: slug, B: slug?} pick map or failing loud on grammar, tag, or lifecycle violations.

nodes.name: _page_js
nodes.summary: Emits the embedded client-side JavaScript that lets the operator pick styleguides and versions and builds the selection code in the browser, mirroring the Python grammar exactly.

nodes.name: render_picker_static_html
nodes.summary: Renders the complete dependency-free static picker page (roster cards, thumbnails, embedded JS encoder) that gets published to the public gh-pages site.

nodes.name: picker_publisher.py
nodes.summary: Publishes the static styleguide picker page plus roster thumbnails to the public GitHub Pages site as a self-contained pack. Thin caller over the shared gh_pages_publish transport that adds the PickerPublishError family, retention pruning, size guard, and verify-after-push so the public site never grows past the GitHub Pages 1 GB cap.

nodes.name: publish_picker
nodes.summary: Builds the picker pack (index.html plus thumbnails), runs sanitation, retention prune and size guard, pushes it to the gh-pages site, and optionally verifies the page is live after push; fail-loud on missing token or git failure.

nodes.name: _git_publish_dir
nodes.summary: Delegates the clone/copy/commit/push of a local pack directory into the public site repo to the shared gh_pages_publish transport, wrapping failures in PickerPublishError.

nodes.name: pre_gate_marcus.py
nodes.summary: Shared pre-gate LLM node invoked once before each conversational pause point (G1, G2C, G3, G4): renders a Jinja2 template-with-slots prompt per gate, calls the marcus chat model, and parses a structured PreFillProposal that the runner threads into the decision card's drafted proposal.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Jinja2 StrictUndefined so any missing template slot fails loud at render time instead of emitting a silently incomplete prompt.

nodes.name: PreFillProposal
nodes.summary: Dataclass carrying the structured pre-fill output of the pre-gate LLM call (proposal text plus metadata) that gets threaded into decision-card drafting.

nodes.name: render_pre_fill_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders the gate-specific Jinja2 template from docs/conversational-gates with the supplied slot values into the pre-fill prompt text.

nodes.name: invoke_pre_gate_marcus
nodes.summary: One LLM call site per gate: renders the slot prompt, invokes the marcus chat model factory, and returns the parsed PreFillProposal without ever promoting a gate from non-blocking to blocking.

nodes.name: _parse_pre_fill_response
nodes.summary: Parses the model's JSON response content into a PreFillProposal, tolerating fenced code blocks and failing loud on malformed payloads.

nodes.name: production_runner.py
nodes.summary: Production-graph runner composition layer — the heart of the Marcus-SPOC runtime. Walks the compiled manifest graph for migrated trials (two node walks: start stops at G1, continuation owns G2B+), dispatching specialists, pausing at HIL gates with decision cards, checkpointing to disk, resuming/recovering trials from verdicts, and recording LangSmith-backed cost evidence per trial.
nodes.languageNotes: Gate-pause side effects (publishers, enrichment, UDAC ratification) must land in BOTH walk bodies — the start walk and _continue_production_walk — a known two-walk trap for this file.

nodes.name: MissingUpstreamContributionError
nodes.summary: Raised when a specialist's declared dependency map references an upstream contribution absent from the production envelope, naming the missing specialist for fail-loud diagnosis.

nodes.name: LegacyEnvelopeSchemaError
nodes.summary: Raised when an on-disk trial envelope uses a legacy schema that the current runner refuses to silently upgrade, forcing an explicit migration decision.

nodes.name: _build_decision_card
nodes.summary: Assembles the gate-specific decision card presented to the operator at a HIL pause: gathers pending nodes, artifact paths, envelope evidence, voice and variant candidates, and the optional pre-gate-marcus drafted proposal.

nodes.name: _write_checkpoint
nodes.summary: Persists the trial's resumable checkpoint (node index, gate, run state, envelope, manifest binding, budget knobs) to the run directory so a paused or crashed trial can be resumed or recovered.

nodes.name: _apply_per_slide_variant_selection
nodes.summary: Applies the operator's per-slide A/B variant picks from run state onto the production envelope, filtering each slide's variants down to exactly the chosen one before downstream dispatch.

nodes.name: _apply_verdict_to_run_state
nodes.summary: Folds an OperatorVerdict (approve/edit/reject plus edit payload) into the RunState at resume time, recording the decision so the continuation walk honors the operator's choice.

nodes.name: apply_llm_execution_mode_overlay
nodes.summary: Overlays the run's opt-in LLM execution mode (e.g., batch transport) onto a specialist's runner payload so dispatch honors the trial-level execution choice.

nodes.name: _runner_payload_for_specialist
nodes.summary: Composes the chartered runner-context payload for each specialist dispatch — directive-derived gamma settings and digests, cluster floors, styleguide resolution, bundle paths — keyed by specialist id and gate code.

nodes.name: _pause_at_gate
nodes.summary: Executes the full HIL gate pause: fires gate-crossing side effects, builds and registers the decision card, persists the conversation turn and checkpoint, emits cost evidence, and returns the paused-at-gate trial envelope.

nodes.name: _dispatch_specialist_at_node
nodes.summary: Shared walk-invariant dispatch site: resolves the specialist's dependency map, UDAC-consumed assets, and runner payload, invokes the dispatch adapter with retry, and appends the resulting contribution to the production envelope.

nodes.name: _pause_at_error
nodes.summary: Converts a specialist dispatch failure into a recoverable paused-at-error trial state: checkpoints progress, records the error context and cost evidence, and returns an envelope the operator can recover from.

nodes.name: _pause_at_provider_batch
nodes.summary: Pauses the trial while a provider-side LLM batch is in flight: persists the batch receipt and checkpoint so resume_batch_production_trial can poll and continue once the provider batch completes.

nodes.name: _dispatch_specialist_catching_batch_wait
nodes.summary: Wraps specialist dispatch to intercept WaitingForProviderBatchError, routing it into the provider-batch pause path instead of the generic error pause.

nodes.name: run_production_trial
nodes.summary: Top-level entry for a new production trial: composes and freezes the manifest for the component selection, validates the directive and corpus, then executes the start node walk (dispatching specialists and firing pre-G1 side effects) until the first gate pause or completion.

nodes.name: resume_production_trial
nodes.summary: Resumes a paused-at-gate trial from an OperatorVerdict: verifies the registered decision card digest, applies the verdict to run state and envelope, and hands off to the continuation walk.

nodes.name: resume_batch_production_trial
nodes.summary: Resumes a trial paused on a provider-side LLM batch: reads the batch receipt, polls or collects the completed batch through the adapter, folds results into the envelope, and continues the walk.

nodes.name: recover_production_trial
nodes.summary: Recovers a paused-at-error trial from its last checkpoint, optionally re-entering at a named node, restoring envelope and run state from disk before continuing the walk.

nodes.name: _continue_production_walk
nodes.summary: The continuation node walk shared by resume, batch-resume, and recover: iterates remaining manifest nodes from the checkpoint index, dispatching specialists, firing gate-crossing side effects (publishers, coverage, UDAC, research wiring), and pausing at each subsequent gate.

nodes.name: research_citation.py
nodes.summary: Citation-facing half of the S3 research wiring: mints cited research entries from accepted Texas retrieval rows with deterministic source_ref and content-stable source_hash, runs the G2 citation-fidelity gate in FAIL mode (a single unsourced citation is RED), and wraps the read-only L2 numeric-provenance audit with the research_supplements channel.

nodes.name: CitationFidelityError
nodes.summary: Raised when the G2 citation-fidelity gate finds unsourced citations, carrying the offending source_refs for the RED verdict.

nodes.name: CitedResearchEntry
nodes.summary: Value object for one minted research citation: citation index, deterministic source_ref, stable source_hash, and the retrieved row's identity/body fields.

nodes.name: mint_cited_entry
nodes.summary: Mints a CitedResearchEntry from an accepted TexasRow, deriving the deterministic source_ref from (provider, source_id) and the stable content hash over the row's identity and body.

nodes.name: count_unsourced_citations
nodes.summary: Counts citations whose source_ref does not resolve to any row in this run's retrieval set — the quantity the G2 gate requires to be exactly zero.

nodes.name: gate_citation_fidelity
nodes.summary: Runs the G2 citation-fidelity gate in FAIL mode: raises CitationFidelityError if any citation's source_ref is unresolvable against the run's retrieval set.

nodes.name: audit_research_supplements
nodes.summary: Calls the shared L2 numeric-provenance audit read-only with retrieved research figures passed as research_supplements, so legitimately sourced figures classify as supplements rather than numeric drift.

nodes.name: assemble_l2_citation_report
nodes.summary: Assembles the combined citation report: the citation manifest, unsourced-citation gate outcome, and the warn-only L2 numeric report, keeping the two checks clearly separated.

nodes.name: dedupe_cited_entries
nodes.summary: Removes duplicate cited entries by source_ref while preserving first-seen citation order, so re-retrieved sources do not inflate the citation manifest.

nodes.name: research_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Runner-invoked orchestration hook keyed to manifest node 04.55 that wires the Irene→Tracy→Texas research bridge through the production walk: derives research-enrichment goals from the locked lesson plan, dispatches retrieval intents to Texas, and mints cited research entries for the S2 workbook producer, degrading deterministically when Scite credentials are absent.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately adds no manifest node — it joins the existing orchestration-handler branch keyed by node id so the pipeline-manifest lockstep regime is never tripped.

nodes.name: DeterministicPostureSelector
nodes.summary: Deterministic retrieval-posture selector that maps each research goal to a provider posture without any LLM call, keeping the Texas dispatch reproducible across runs.

nodes.name: _research_goals_from_raw
nodes.summary: Extracts research-enrichment goals from the raw locked lesson plan's in-scope plan units, normalizing goal text and unit binding.

nodes.name: has_research_goals
nodes.summary: Cheap predicate over the production envelope's locked plan: true when any in-scope plan unit carries a research-enrichment goal, gating whether the wiring runs at all.

nodes.name: _dispatch_intents_to_texas
nodes.summary: Dispatches the derived retrieval intents through the Texas retrieval contract, collecting accepted rows and describing dropped results for the audit trail.

nodes.name: run_research_wiring
nodes.summary: Main hook the runner invokes at the plan-lock node: checks goals and credentials, dispatches intents to Texas (live or injected), mints cited entries, builds resolvable source refs, and attaches the research contribution — or a deterministic degrade record — to the envelope.

nodes.name: research_entries_from_envelope
nodes.summary: Reads the minted cited research entries back out of the production envelope's research contribution for downstream consumers like the workbook producer.

nodes.name: routing.py
nodes.summary: Manifest-driven specialist routing: resolves the next step transition from the PipelineManifest into a RoutingDecision carrying the target specialist, optional dispatch envelope, and decision-card schema.

nodes.name: RoutingDecision
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic value object for one manifest-resolved step transition: current and next node ids, target specialist, dispatch envelope, and decision-card schema.

nodes.name: route_step
nodes.summary: Resolves the outgoing edge for the current node in the PipelineManifest and returns the RoutingDecision for the next step, failing loud on unknown or ambiguous transitions.

nodes.name: slide_variant_selection.py
nodes.summary: Parses the Storyboard-A chooser selection code (SBA-<run_tag>-1:A 2:B ...) that the operator pastes back as the G2B verdict edit_payload into a slide_id→variant map, and fail-louds on any coverage gap so a partial or stale pick can never silently default downstream.

nodes.name: parse_selection_code
nodes.summary: Parses the SBA chooser code into a {slide_id: variant} map keyed by slide_id (not index), raising SlideVariantSelectionError on malformed grammar, duplicate indices, or out-of-range slides.

nodes.name: validate_selections
nodes.summary: Enforces the total-coverage invariant: exactly one chosen variant per slide, every referenced slide known, every chosen variant actually present in the variants-by-slide map.

nodes.name: specialist_summary_writer.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrator-facing barrel that re-exports the specialist summary writer API from app.models.state.specialist_summary_artifacts, so specialist graphs can call the emit hook without importing app.marcus and breaking lane contracts.

nodes.name: styleguide_picker.py
nodes.summary: Pre-run localhost HTML style-picker at CD-entry: serves a card-grid page rendered from the CD-owned styleguide SSOT over an ephemeral-port http.server, captures the operator's pick via a single same-origin POST, and writes it into the run directive's gamma_settings plus a styleguide_picker_provenance audit block and an append-only pick-events sidecar. Single-variant (A-only or B-only) picks are legitimate.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an OS-level advisory file lock plus atomic temp-file-rename writes so the directive read-modify-write and the sidecar append survive concurrent pickers.

nodes.name: _advisory_lock
nodes.summary: Context manager taking an OS-level advisory lock on a store path so directive and sidecar mutations are serialized across concurrent picker processes.

nodes.name: read_pick_events
nodes.summary: Reads the append-only pick-events sidecar (JSONL), tolerating partial trailing lines, and returns the parsed event history used for last-used ordering and dedup.

nodes.name: load_picker_roster
nodes.summary: Builds the picker roster from the CD-owned styleguide SSOT: applies lifecycle/visibility filtering (probes, deprecated), attaches last-used metadata from pick events, and validates slug membership.

nodes.name: _render_card
nodes.summary: Renders one styleguide's HTML card (thumbnail with honesty fallbacks, lifecycle badges, pick controls) for the local card-grid page; the CSS here is the single source the static gh-pages emitter also imports.

nodes.name: render_picker_html
nodes.summary: Renders the full local picker page: roster card grid with A/B slot selection posting to the relative /pick action served by the ephemeral localhost server.

nodes.name: _PickHandler
nodes.summary: http.server request handler that serves the picker page and thumbnails same-origin, accepts exactly one valid POST to /pick, hands the decoded pick to the capture callback, and then shuts the server down.

nodes.name: capture_pick
nodes.summary: Runs the pick-capture ceremony: starts the ephemeral-port localhost server, opens the browser to the same-origin page, waits (with timeout) for one valid pick POST, and falls back to the CLI-numbered flow when the browser path is unavailable.

nodes.name: write_pick_to_directive
nodes.summary: Writes the committed pick into the run directive's gamma_settings[] with existing A/B semantics verbatim plus a styleguide_picker_provenance audit block, under the advisory lock with an atomic read-modify-write so the run's own input artifact carries the audit trail.

nodes.name: append_pick_event
nodes.summary: Appends a deduplicated pick event (picks, course, run id, timestamp) to the append-only JSONL sidecar under the advisory lock, feeding last-used ordering and cross-run pick history.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that loads the roster, runs the pick-capture ceremony, and writes the resulting pick to the target directive plus the events sidecar.

nodes.name: supervisor.py
nodes.summary: Preset-based orchestration supervisor adapting the Slab 3 FR27 preset switch: production maps to plan_and_execute, explore maps to react, stepping the manifest via route_step without disturbing the legacy lesson-planner flow.

nodes.name: Supervisor
nodes.summary: Thin supervisor over a PipelineManifest exposing the preset-derived mode and a run_step that delegates each node transition to route_step.

nodes.name: udac_wiring.py
nodes.summary: UDAC v1 orchestrator wiring: the gate-crossing run-asset-index WRITER (record_gate_ratification, fired in both walk bodies, disk-primary and monotonically reconciled) and the dispatch-site READER (resolve_consumed_assets), behind an env feature flag that defaults OFF so the existing pipeline stays byte-identical. All RAI model logic lives in the neutral run_asset_index module.
nodes.languageNotes: Disk-primary reconcile pattern: the writer loads the on-disk RAI and merges monotonically so a continuation or recover walk re-crossing a gate rehydrates prior ratified_at timestamps instead of re-stamping.

nodes.name: udac_active
nodes.summary: Env feature-flag predicate gating all UDAC v1 behavior; default OFF keeps the existing pipeline byte-identical.

nodes.name: record_gate_ratification
nodes.summary: Gate-crossing side effect that assembles/updates the run-asset-index on disk: loads the existing RAI, ratifies the assets cleared by this gate, reconciles monotonically, and writes it back — idempotent across both walk bodies.

nodes.name: resolve_consumed_assets
nodes.summary: Dispatch-site reader that resolves the assets a specialist declares it consumes against the on-disk run-asset-index, failing loud when a declared asset is missing or unratified.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the runtime-owned Pydantic models: AdhocResponse/TokenCount, ProductionEnvelope/SpecialistContribution, ProductionTrialEnvelope, and the TrialEconomicsReport family.

nodes.name: adhoc_response.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic response contract for one-shot Marcus ad-hoc asks: the AdhocResponse payload plus a TokenCount usage model that enforces total_tokens covering input plus output.

nodes.name: TokenCount
nodes.summary: Strict token-usage model for the ad-hoc LLM call with non-negative fields and a model validator enforcing total_tokens >= input_tokens + output_tokens.

nodes.name: AdhocResponse
nodes.summary: Frozen response envelope for a one-shot Marcus ad-hoc ask carrying the answer text, model id, and TokenCount usage.

nodes.name: production_envelope.py
nodes.summary: Strict cross-specialist accumulator for production composition: SpecialistContribution entries with SHA-256 output digests and timezone-aware timestamps, collected in a ProductionEnvelope that supports contribution lookup, latest-for-specialist queries, append, and node-scoped drops.
nodes.languageNotes: Output digests are computed over canonical JSON (sorted keys, ASCII, no NaN) so contribution integrity checks are hashseed- and platform-independent.

nodes.name: compute_output_digest
nodes.summary: Returns a stable SHA-256 digest over the canonical-JSON serialization of a specialist output payload, the integrity anchor every contribution carries.

nodes.name: SpecialistContribution
nodes.summary: One specialist's recorded output in the envelope: node id, specialist id, output payload, and a validator-enforced digest plus timezone-aware contributed_at, with a from_output factory that mints the digest.

nodes.name: ProductionEnvelope
nodes.summary: The accumulating cross-specialist envelope for one trial: UUID4-enforced trial id and ordered contributions with get_contribution, latest_for_specialist, add_contribution, and drop_contributions_from_nodes operations.

nodes.name: production_trial_envelope.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic envelope for production-graph trial lifecycle state (registered, in-flight, paused-at-gate, and terminal statuses) wrapping the ProductionEnvelope with timezone-aware started/completed timestamps, UUID4 enforcement, and cross-field lifecycle invariants.

nodes.name: ProductionTrialEnvelope
nodes.summary: Trial lifecycle record: status literal, gate position, embedded ProductionEnvelope, and validators enforcing UUID4 trial id, timezone-aware timestamps, and status-consistent lifecycle invariants (e.g., completed_at only on terminal states).

nodes.name: trial_economics_report.py
nodes.summary: Strict per-trial economics report models: per-agent cost attribution entries, cost-drift alerts against history, budget status, and the aggregate TrialEconomicsReport with timezone-aware timestamps and non-negative model-cost invariants.

nodes.name: AgentCostEntry
nodes.summary: Per-agent aggregate cost attribution for one trial: agent name, assigned model, call count, token totals, and cost in USD, all non-negative by validation.

nodes.name: DriftAlert
nodes.summary: One per-agent cost-drift finding comparing this trial's spend against the historical baseline, carrying the drift ratio and severity.

nodes.name: BudgetStatus
nodes.summary: Trial budget check result: running total versus budget with an ok/warn/exceeded style status literal.

nodes.name: TrialEconomicsReport
nodes.summary: The aggregate per-trial economics report: total cost, per-agent entries, drift alerts, budget status, and trace linkage, with validators enforcing timezone-aware timestamps and non-negative model costs.

nodes.name: component_selection.py
nodes.summary: ComponentSelection — the canonical record of which pipeline components a run composes at compile time before the freeze. Lives on RunState so the resume/recover walk rehydrates the selection from the frozen run record and never re-defaults; canonical-JSON bytes make the selection hashable into the content-addressed digest in a hashseed-independent way.

nodes.name: ComponentSelection
nodes.summary: Frozen selection record with a production_default factory, as_map/selected_components views, and canonical_bytes serialization (sorted keys, stable encoding) used in the two-part content-addressed manifest digest.

nodes.name: operator_verdict.py
nodes.summary: OperatorVerdict — the frozen value object for HIL gate decisions (approve/edit/reject, FR31 and FR34): UUID4 and timezone enforcement, SHA-256 decision-card digest binding, triple-layer tamper-verb rejection, and verb-payload consistency, plus a small CLI to emit its JSON schema.
nodes.languageNotes: Tamper-evidence is triple-layered: Literal field typing, a re-check validator that also fires on validate_assignment mutations and model_construct paths, and a schema-pin test.

nodes.name: OperatorVerdict
nodes.summary: Frozen HIL verdict: verb literal (approve/edit/reject), gate and trial binding, SHA-256 decision-card digest, and validators enforcing UUID4 ids, timezone-aware timestamps, digest shape, tamper-verb rejection, and verb-payload consistency.

nodes.name: emit_operator_verdict_schema
nodes.summary: Writes the OperatorVerdict JSON schema to the given path, backing the schema-pin third layer of the tamper-evidence guarantee.

nodes.name: specialist_summary_artifacts.py
nodes.summary: Specialist summary persistence for adjacent-gate context: canonicalizes specialist ids against the compiler's alias map, builds length-validated summary text from run state (received keys, decisions, artifact paths, resolution trail), emits summaries into the trial's runs directory, and reloads the most recent summary before a gate.

nodes.name: AdjacentSummary
nodes.summary: Dataclass pairing a loaded summary's text with its source path and timestamp, the unit returned when adjacent gate context is rehydrated.

nodes.name: canonical_specialist_id
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes a specialist id through the manifest compiler's SPECIALIST_ALIASES map, failing loud on ids outside the canonical or deferred rosters.

nodes.name: build_summary_text
nodes.summary: Builds the human-readable specialist summary block (gate, timestamp, received keys, decisions, artifacts, resolution trail) and validates it against the summary length ceiling.

nodes.name: _artifacts_from_contribution
nodes.summary: Extracts artifact paths from a specialist's envelope contribution across the known output shapes so the summary lists real produced artifacts.

nodes.name: emit_summary
nodes.summary: Writes one specialist summary file into the trial's summary directory with a timestamped filename, the persistence hook specialist graphs call at gate crossings.

nodes.name: emit_summary_for_state
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper that derives received keys, decisions, artifacts, and the resolution trail from a run state dict and emits the summary in one call.

nodes.name: load_most_recent_summary
nodes.summary: Loads the most recent summary for a trial (optionally before a cutoff timestamp) as an AdjacentSummary, giving the next gate its adjacent specialist context.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_validators.py
nodes.summary: Standalone cross-field validators for OperatorVerdict (FR31/FR34 tamper-evidence): rejects tamper verbs (timeout, auto_approve) even on paths that bypass the Literal type, and enforces verb-payload consistency (edit requires edit_payload, reject requires reject_reason, approve forbids both).

nodes.name: enforce_no_tamper_verbs
nodes.summary: Second layer of the triple-layer FR34 red-rejection: raises on tamper verbs (timeout, auto_approve) so validate_assignment mutations and model_construct ingest paths cannot smuggle a machine verdict.

nodes.name: enforce_verb_payload_consistency
nodes.summary: Enforces verb-payload pairing: edit requires a non-None edit_payload, reject requires reject_reason, and approve with either field set is rejected as a likely typo.

nodes.name: cascade_config.py
nodes.summary: Loaders for the operator-editable model-cascade and OpenAI pricing YAML configs used by economics: parses with content digests, canonicalizes agent names across hyphen/underscore variants, rejects alias collisions, verifies pricing covers every cascade model, and exposes a small validation CLI.

nodes.name: normalize_agent_name
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes agent identifiers across hyphen/underscore and case variants so cascade lookups and cost attribution agree on one spelling.

nodes.name: CascadeConfig
nodes.summary: Validated model-cascade table mapping agents to assigned models with alias support, a collision-rejecting validator, and resolve_entry lookup by normalized agent name.

nodes.name: PricingTable
nodes.summary: Per-model token pricing table with compute_cost turning input/output token counts into USD for a given model id.

nodes.name: load_cascade
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the model-cascade YAML into a CascadeConfig, attaching the file's SHA-256 content digest for provenance.

nodes.name: load_pricing
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the OpenAI pricing YAML into a PricingTable, attaching the file's SHA-256 content digest for provenance.

nodes.name: ensure_pricing_covers_cascade
nodes.summary: Fails loud if any model assigned in the cascade lacks a pricing entry, so cost measurement can never silently price a call at zero.

nodes.name: economics.py
nodes.summary: Per-trial cost measurement and persistence for the migrated runtime: fetches the trial's LangSmith trace, attributes billable LLM runs to agents via the cascade config and prices them with the pricing table, checks the trial budget, computes per-agent drift against report history, and renders/records markdown cost reports under the runs root.

nodes.name: _fetch_trial_trace
nodes.summary: Fetches the trial's run tree from the LangSmith client filtered by trial id, normalizing the provider objects into the internal trace shape used for cost extraction.

nodes.name: check_trial_budget
nodes.summary: Compares the trial's running USD total to its budget and returns the BudgetStatus (within, warning, or exceeded).

nodes.name: compute_per_agent_drift
nodes.summary: Computes per-agent cost drift for this trial against the median of historical reports, emitting DriftAlert entries when an agent's spend deviates beyond threshold.

nodes.name: measure_trial_cost
nodes.summary: The main measurement pipeline: obtains the trace (live client, injected root, or fixture), filters billable LLM runs, attributes each to an agent via the cascade, prices tokens with the pricing table, and assembles the TrialEconomicsReport with budget status and drift alerts.

nodes.name: _render_cost_report_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders a TrialEconomicsReport into the human-readable markdown cost report (per-agent table, budget line, drift alerts, trace links) written into the run directory.

nodes.name: record_trial_cost_report
nodes.summary: Persists the trial's economics report as both canonical JSON and rendered markdown under the runs root, the evidence artifact the runner emits at pauses and completion.

nodes.name: summarize_cost_reports
nodes.summary: Aggregates recorded cost reports across trials under the runs root into a cross-trial summary (totals and recency), used for history-aware drift baselines and operator review.

nodes.name: load_trace_fixture
nodes.summary: Loads a JSON trace fixture from disk into the internal trace shape so cost measurement can run offline against captured traces.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Declares CONSUMED_PAYLOAD_KEYS, the frozen vocabulary of every envelope payload key Gary's act body reads. The Ratchet-D manifest contract lockstep test pins manifest dependency edges targeting gary and the S3 package builder's output keys to this set, locking the lesson-plan → slide-briefs → Gamma chain into one vocabulary.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel entry point for the LiteLLM Batch transport package, re-exporting the adapter, errors, join utilities, JSONL encoding, and receipt persistence for Marcus-SPOC batch mode. Its docstring binds the package to the provider Batch API path and explicitly forbids confusing it with litellm.batch_completion.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel with an explicit __all__ and a binding research-doc reference in the module docstring.

nodes.name: adapter.py
nodes.summary: LiteLLM Files + Batches SDK adapter implementing the provider Batch API lifecycle: upload JSONL, create/retrieve/cancel batches, download output, and the composite submit_and_receipt / join_completed_output operations. Guards at construction time against accidentally binding the synchronous litellm.batch_completion helper.
nodes.languageNotes: Dependency-injected callables (create_file, create_batch, etc.) allow test doubles without mocking the LiteLLM SDK module directly.

nodes.name: cost_report.py
nodes.summary: Hermetic batch cost/latency reporting (B4): aggregates token usage from joined Batch output rows plus receipt timestamps and prices them via the cascade pricing table, without calling LiteLLM Enterprise billing APIs. Includes a fail-soft emitter that never blocks the production run on report errors.

nodes.name: errors.py
nodes.summary: Exception types for the LLM batch transport: LlmBatchError for general batch failures and WaitingForProviderBatchError, a control-flow signal raised when a submitted provider batch is still pending and the run should pause/resume later.

nodes.name: join.py
nodes.summary: Joins provider Batch output JSONL rows back to their expected custom_ids, producing a JoinResult that tracks per-row success, missing rows, and unexpected rows. Also parses raw output JSONL text into row dicts.

nodes.name: jsonl.py
nodes.summary: Batch input JSONL construction helpers: builds /v1/chat/completions request rows with custom_ids, estimates encoded byte size, and enforces the provider upload size budget before encoding.

nodes.name: prompt_cache.py
nodes.summary: Derives OpenAI prompt-cache keys from configured strategies and resolves the vision node's cache key from the LLM execution config, plus helpers to attach the key to request extra_body and to extract cached-token counts from usage payloads.

nodes.name: receipts.py
nodes.summary: BatchReceipt Pydantic model and durable receipt persistence: writes/reads per-run batch receipts under the runs root and normalizes heterogeneous provider batch objects (dict or SDK object) into the receipt schema.
nodes.languageNotes: normalize_batch_object uses a local _get closure to read fields uniformly from dicts or SDK objects, keeping the receipt schema stable across LiteLLM return shapes.

nodes.name: llm_batch_eligibility.py
nodes.summary: Batch eligibility matrix (A3): Pydantic models loading a YAML matrix that declares which call sites are batch-eligible and v1-batch-routable, with validators requiring the vision site to be v1-routed and consistency between routing and eligibility flags.

nodes.name: llm_execution_config.py
nodes.summary: LLM execution mode configuration: Pydantic models for per-node realtime/batch transport profiles (provider, model, token limits, prompt-cache strategy) loaded from YAML, with a validator requiring a vision node entry and profile resolution by execution mode.

nodes.name: batch_route.py
nodes.summary: Vision Batch route (B2): end-to-end LiteLLM multimodal Batch flow for 07G slide perception — builds per-slide chat-completions rows, submits or resumes via receipt, polls to terminal status (or raises WaitingForProviderBatchError under the pause wait policy), joins outputs, emits the cost report, and parses each slide's assistant text into VisionProviderResponse objects. Active only when the payload's llm_execution_mode is exactly 'batch' and the eligibility matrix marks vision v1-routable.

nodes.name: LiteLlmBatchAdapter
nodes.summary: Adapter over LiteLLM's Files + Batches SDK callables covering the full batch lifecycle: upload_batch_jsonl, create/retrieve/cancel batch, download output, submit_and_receipt (submit then persist a normalized receipt), and join_completed_output (download and join output/error files).

nodes.name: _coerce_file_bytes
nodes.summary: Coerces heterogeneous file_content return shapes (bytes, str, readable objects, HttpxBinaryResponseContent-like) into raw bytes, raising LlmBatchError on unsupported types.

nodes.name: _usage_from_row
nodes.summary: Extracts prompt/completion/total token usage from a joined batch row's raw response body, tolerating both prompt_tokens/input_tokens naming variants and returning None on malformed rows.

nodes.name: build_batch_cost_report
nodes.summary: Builds the batch cost report dict: aggregates token usage across joined rows, prices it with the cascade pricing table, computes submitted-to-completed latency from receipt timestamps, and records row counts plus explanatory notes.

nodes.name: write_batch_cost_report
nodes.summary: Persists a batch cost report as cost-report.json under the run's batch directory, creating directories as needed.

nodes.name: emit_batch_cost_report_fail_soft
nodes.summary: Fail-soft wrapper that builds and writes the batch cost report, swallowing any exception so cost reporting can never block the production run.

nodes.name: LlmBatchError
nodes.summary: Base exception for LLM batch transport failures, carrying a message describing the batch lifecycle step that failed.

nodes.name: WaitingForProviderBatchError
nodes.summary: Control-flow exception raised when a provider batch has been submitted but is not yet terminal, carrying run/batch identifiers so the orchestrator can pause the run and resume it after the batch completes.

nodes.name: JoinedBatchRow
nodes.summary: Immutable record for one joined batch output row: custom_id, ok flag, response body, error payload, and the raw row dict.

nodes.name: JoinResult
nodes.summary: Aggregate join outcome keyed by custom_id, tracking arrival order plus missing and unexpected ids, with ok_ids/failed_ids convenience accessors.

nodes.name: _row_ok
nodes.summary: Determines whether a raw batch output row succeeded by checking the absence of an error payload and a 2xx response status code.

nodes.name: join_output_rows
nodes.summary: Joins raw output rows to the expected custom_id set, raising LlmBatchError on missing custom_ids or duplicates, and returns a JoinResult with per-row ok status, missing ids, and unexpected ids.

nodes.name: parse_output_jsonl
nodes.summary: Parses batch output JSONL text (bytes or str) into a list of row dicts, skipping blank lines and non-dict entries.

nodes.name: estimate_jsonl_bytes
nodes.summary: Estimates the encoded byte size of a JSONL payload by summing each row's compact JSON encoding plus newline bytes.

nodes.name: assert_within_size_budget
nodes.summary: Raises LlmBatchError when the estimated JSONL payload size exceeds the provider upload budget, reporting actual versus allowed bytes.

nodes.name: encode_batch_jsonl
nodes.summary: Encodes request rows into a newline-terminated JSONL byte payload after enforcing the size budget.

nodes.name: make_chat_completions_row
nodes.summary: Builds one Batch API request row targeting /v1/chat/completions with a custom_id, model, messages, optional max_completion_tokens/temperature, and merged extra_body fields such as prompt-cache keys.

nodes.name: derive_prompt_cache_key
nodes.summary: Maps a configured prompt-cache-key strategy name to a concrete cache key for a node, falling back to treating unknown strategies as literal keys.

nodes.name: resolve_vision_prompt_cache_key
nodes.summary: Loads the LLM execution config, resolves the vision node's transport profile for the requested mode, and derives its prompt-cache key, raising ValueError on invalid modes.

nodes.name: prompt_cache_extra_body
nodes.summary: Wraps a prompt-cache key into the extra_body dict shape expected by chat-completions request rows, returning an empty dict for a null key.

nodes.name: extract_cached_tokens
nodes.summary: Extracts the cached-token count from a usage payload's prompt_tokens_details, tolerating missing or malformed structures by returning None.

nodes.name: BatchReceipt
nodes.summary: Pydantic model of a durable provider-batch receipt: run/batch/file ids, status, endpoint, provider, model, completion window, submitted/completed timestamps, row and request counts, and metadata.

nodes.name: receipt_dir
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical per-run batch receipt directory under the runs root.

nodes.name: write_receipt
nodes.summary: Serializes a BatchReceipt to JSON and writes it to the run's receipt path, creating directories as needed, and returns the written path.

nodes.name: read_receipt
nodes.summary: Reads and validates a persisted BatchReceipt for a run id from disk.

nodes.name: utc_now_iso
nodes.summary: Returns the current UTC time as a Z-suffixed ISO-8601 string with second precision, used for receipt timestamps.

nodes.name: normalize_batch_object
nodes.summary: Normalizes a provider batch object (dict or SDK object) into a BatchReceipt, reading fields via a uniform accessor and stamping submitted_at when the provider omits it.

nodes.name: EligibilitySite
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for one call site's batch eligibility: batch_eligible, v1_routed, criteria_met, and rationale, with a validator forbidding v1 routing of a batch-ineligible site.

nodes.name: BatchEligibilityMatrix
nodes.summary: Top-level eligibility matrix keyed by site name with version and criteria, validating that the vision site exists and is v1-routed, and exposing lookups for batch eligibility and v1 batch routability.

nodes.name: load_batch_eligibility
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the batch eligibility matrix from its YAML config file into a BatchEligibilityMatrix.

nodes.name: NodeTransportProfile
nodes.summary: Pydantic model of one transport profile for a node: provider, model, max_completion_tokens, reasoning effort, verbosity, prompt-cache strategy/retention, and batch fallback family plus baseline batch id.

nodes.name: NodeExecutionProfile
nodes.summary: Per-node execution entry holding default_mode plus realtime and batch NodeTransportProfiles, with profile_for(mode) selecting the transport profile and rejecting unknown modes.

nodes.name: LlmExecutionConfig
nodes.summary: Root LLM execution config with version, default_mode, and node map; validates that a vision node exists with both realtime and batch profiles, and resolves a node's transport profile for an optional mode override.

nodes.name: load_llm_execution
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the LLM execution YAML config into an LlmExecutionConfig.

nodes.name: resolve_vision_execution_mode
nodes.summary: Reads llm_execution_mode from the specialist payload, defaulting to realtime; only the exact string 'batch' switches the vision route on.

nodes.name: _assert_unique_slide_ids
nodes.summary: Validates that every slide has a non-empty, unique slide_id, raising VisionProviderError on blanks or duplicates before batch rows are built.

nodes.name: build_vision_batch_rows
nodes.summary: Builds one chat-completions Batch row per slide: resolves the prompt-cache key, constructs multimodal perception messages via the vision provider, and assigns deterministic run/slide custom_ids.

nodes.name: extract_assistant_text
nodes.summary: Walks a joined batch row's raw response -> body -> choices -> message structure to extract the assistant text content, raising VisionProviderError with the custom_id on any malformed level.

nodes.name: poll_until_terminal
nodes.summary: Polls the adapter for batch status at a fixed interval until a terminal status (completed/failed/expired/cancelled) or timeout, returning a normalized BatchReceipt or raising LlmBatchError on timeout.

nodes.name: run_vision_batch_perception
nodes.summary: End-to-end vision batch perception: verifies eligibility and slide-id uniqueness, resumes from a persisted receipt or submits a new batch, either pauses via WaitingForProviderBatchError or polls to completion, joins outputs, emits the fail-soft cost report, and parses each slide's assistant text into VisionProviderResponse results in input order.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Section 02A LLM-driven directive composer, re-exporting the composer entry points (compose, write_directive_yaml), the ComposerCache, and the Directive model family as the public API.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel using explicit __all__ to pin the public surface.

nodes.name: _cache.py
nodes.summary: Minimal in-memory response cache for the Section 02A composer, mapping normalized-prompt cache keys to LLM classification responses so equivalent prompts reuse prior answers.

nodes.name: ComposerCache
nodes.summary: Dict-backed get/set cache keyed by SHA256 prompt digests, letting the composer skip repeated LLM invocations for identical classification prompts.

nodes.name: _prompt.py
nodes.summary: Prompt rendering and cache-key normalization for Section 02A: renders the Jinja2 corpus-classification prompt and strips volatile identity (operator_id, timestamps, UUID4 run_ids) before SHA256-hashing so semantically equal prompts share a cache key.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Jinja2 with StrictUndefined against a template under docs/conversational-gates, and regex-based volatile-token stripping for deterministic cache keys.

nodes.name: render_classification_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders the section-02a-composer.j2 template into a per-file classification prompt, including file metadata, corpus summary, and a hex sample for binary files.

nodes.name: normalize_prompt
nodes.summary: Strips volatile tokens (operator ids, ISO timestamps, UUID4 values) from a prompt via regex substitution so cache keys are stable across runs.

nodes.name: cache_key_for_prompt
nodes.summary: Computes the SHA256 hex digest of the normalized prompt, producing the cache key used by ComposerCache.

nodes.name: corpus_summary_for
nodes.summary: Builds a newline-joined listing of corpus-relative file paths used as shared context inside every classification prompt.

nodes.name: cli_adapter.py
nodes.summary: CLI-side adapter bridging the Section 02A composer's compose/write_directive_yaml API to the legacy path/digest call shape expected by trial CLI callers, including model resolution trail persistence and directive SHA256 digest computation.

nodes.name: compose_and_write
nodes.summary: Validates the corpus directory, resolves or accepts a chat model, writes a model_resolution_trail.json audit record, runs compose, writes the directive YAML, and returns the path plus its SHA256 digest.

nodes.name: composer.py
nodes.summary: Core LLM-driven Section 02A directive composition: walks a lesson corpus leaf, applies rule-based exclusions, classifies each remaining file via a cached LangChain chat-model call, and assembles a validated Directive that can be serialized to YAML.
nodes.languageNotes: Fail-loud design: raises NonRunnableScopeError when pointed at a course/module container root instead of a lesson corpus leaf, enforcing the lesson-scoped corpus contract.

nodes.name: DirectiveCompositionError
nodes.summary: RuntimeError subclass raised when the Section 02A corpus cannot be composed (missing directory, malformed LLM payload, empty corpus).

nodes.name: NonRunnableScopeError
nodes.summary: DirectiveCompositionError subclass raised when composition is pointed at a course/module container root rather than a lesson corpus leaf.

nodes.name: assert_lesson_corpus_leaf
nodes.summary: Fails loudly with NonRunnableScopeError when the corpus directory contains course/module sentinel markers (course.yaml, module.yaml, modules/), guarding against running composition at container scope.

nodes.name: _walk_corpus_files
nodes.summary: Validates the corpus directory exists and is a lesson leaf, then returns a sorted recursive listing of all files under it.

nodes.name: _source_from_payload
nodes.summary: Constructs a DirectiveSource from a parsed LLM classification payload, mapping role, description, and expected_min_words fields.

nodes.name: _ignored_source
nodes.summary: Builds a DirectiveSource marked with an excluded_reason for files rejected by rule-based exclusion, preserving them in the directive for auditability.

nodes.name: _classify_with_llm
nodes.summary: Classifies a single corpus file: renders the classification prompt, checks the ComposerCache by normalized cache key, invokes the chat model on a miss, caches the response, and parses it into a DirectiveSource.

nodes.name: compose
nodes.summary: Top-level composition entry point: walks the corpus, applies rule-based exclusions, LLM-classifies remaining files with per-run ref_ids, and assembles a timezone-aware, UUID4-stamped Directive.

nodes.name: write_directive_yaml
nodes.summary: Serializes a Directive to YAML via model_dump and yaml.safe_dump, creating parent directories and returning the written path.

nodes.name: directive_model.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model family for the Section 02A directive: role and excluded-reason vocabularies, the per-file DirectiveSource with role/field invariants and legacy-key migration, and the top-level Directive with UUID4 and timezone-aware validators.
