nodes.languageNotes: Follows the repo's Pydantic v2 checklist: closed-enum rejection via TypeAdapter literal validation, enforce_uuid4_version/enforce_tz_aware field validators, and a model_dump override that excludes internal fields.

nodes.name: DirectiveRole
nodes.summary: Closed vocabulary of directive source roles used to classify corpus files within a Section 02A directive.

nodes.name: ExcludedReason
nodes.summary: Closed vocabulary of reasons a corpus file may be excluded from directive composition (e.g., rule-based operational-file exclusion).

nodes.name: DirectiveSource
nodes.summary: Per-file directive entry carrying ref_id, locator, provider, role, description, and expected_min_words; validators migrate the legacy source_id key, reject unknown roles/excluded reasons, and enforce role-conditional field invariants.

nodes.name: Directive
nodes.summary: Top-level Section 02A directive model with run_id (enforced UUID4), corpus_dir, composed sources, timezone-aware composed_at, schema_version, and gamma_settings; overrides model_dump to trim internal fields.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Section 02A G0 poll surface, re-exporting SURFACE_ID and the directive display, edit, verdict-submission, and resume helpers.

nodes.name: _transports.py
nodes.summary: In-process transport handlers (CLI, HTTP, MCP-stdio) for the Section 02A G0 poll surface; each wraps the shared submit_verdict pipeline and returns a uniform transport-tagged response with directive display, verdict, and resume result.
nodes.languageNotes: The three transports intentionally share one code path (_transport_response), keeping CLI/HTTP/MCP behavior in parity by construction.

nodes.name: _transport_response
nodes.summary: Shared response builder that packages the transport name, directive display card, verdict dump, and resume_from_verdict result into one payload.

nodes.name: submit_cli_verdict
nodes.summary: CLI transport entry: forwards an operator verdict (approve/edit/reject) into the shared submit_verdict pipeline and returns the uniform transport response.

nodes.name: submit_http_verdict
nodes.summary: HTTP transport entry: forwards an operator verdict into the shared submit_verdict pipeline and returns the uniform transport response.

nodes.name: submit_mcp_stdio_verdict
nodes.summary: MCP-stdio transport entry: forwards an operator verdict into the shared submit_verdict pipeline and returns the uniform transport response.

nodes.name: poll_surface.py
nodes.summary: Canonical Section 02A G0 poll-surface: loads and displays directives with canonical SHA256 decision-card digests, applies field-level operator edits to whitelisted DirectiveSource fields, validates verdict submission, and resumes runs only when the verdict digest matches the (possibly edited) directive.
nodes.languageNotes: Registers a parity contract mandating cli/http/mcp-stdio transports; digest matching over canonical JSON bytes prevents resuming from a stale or tampered decision card.

nodes.name: canonical_model_bytes
nodes.summary: Serializes a Pydantic model to canonical JSON bytes (sorted keys, compact separators, ASCII) so digests are stable across processes.

nodes.name: compute_model_digest
nodes.summary: Returns the SHA256 hex digest of a model's canonical bytes; used as the decision-card digest binding verdicts to directive content.

nodes.name: load_directive
nodes.summary: Reads a directive YAML file and validates it into a Directive model via model_validate.

nodes.name: display_directive
nodes.summary: Produces the operator-facing decision card: the directive dump paired with its computed decision-card digest and surface id.

nodes.name: apply_directive_edit
nodes.summary: Applies a DirectiveEditPayload to a directive: resolves each ref_id to its source row, rejects unknown ref_ids and non-whitelisted fields, applies updates, and re-validates the whole Directive, wrapping validation failures in GateError.

nodes.name: _directive_for_verdict
nodes.summary: Resolves the effective directive for a verdict verb: the original for approve/reject, the edited directive for edit (requiring an edit payload).

nodes.name: submit_verdict
nodes.summary: Builds a validated Section02AOperatorVerdict for approve/edit/reject: coerces the directive, normalizes the edit payload, computes the effective directive, and stamps the decision-card digest of what the operator approved.

nodes.name: resume_from_verdict
nodes.summary: Gate-resume guard: recomputes the effective directive's digest and refuses to resume (GateError) on run_id or digest mismatch, returning the resumed directive and verdict dumps only when the verdict provably matches.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_02a.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 models for Section 02A HIL poll-surface verdicts: DirectiveEditPayload (field edits keyed by source ref_id) and Section02AOperatorVerdict with UUID4, timezone-aware, SHA256-digest, and verb/payload consistency validators.
nodes.languageNotes: Verb/payload consistency is enforced at model level: edit requires an edit_payload, reject requires a reject_reason, approve forbids both.

nodes.name: DirectiveEditPayload
nodes.summary: Field-level edit map keyed by DirectiveSource ref_id; a validator rejects empty update blocks so an edit verdict always carries substantive changes.

nodes.name: Section02AOperatorVerdict
nodes.summary: Operator verdict record (approve/edit/reject) binding run_id, surface_id, operator, submission time, and the SHA256 decision-card digest; validators enforce UUID4, timezone awareness, digest format, and verb/payload consistency.

nodes.name: canonical_asset_record.v0_1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v0.1 JSON Schema for the canonical asset record — the course-source evidence unit carrying asset kind (reading, lecture, lab, etc.), grounding status (source_grounded through needs_sme_review), source refs with locators, and SME-review flags.
nodes.languageNotes: Emitted from a Pydantic v2 model; uses $defs enums with additionalProperties:false for closed-shape validation.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel package init that re-exports the Marcus dispatch contract surface (DispatchEnvelope, DispatchReceipt, kinds, outcomes, and both builder functions) under the app namespace.

nodes.name: contract.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Marcus-to-specialist dispatch contract: DispatchKind/DispatchOutcome enums, DispatchEnvelope and DispatchReceipt payload models, and builder helpers that stamp correlation IDs and UTC timestamps.

nodes.name: DispatchKind
nodes.summary: Enum of the dispatch categories Marcus can route to specialists (the closed set of specialist work kinds).

nodes.name: DispatchOutcome
nodes.summary: Enum of terminal dispatch result states reported back in a DispatchReceipt.

nodes.name: DispatchEnvelope
nodes.summary: Outbound dispatch payload model carrying run_id, specialist_id, dispatch kind, input packet, context refs, correlation_id, and a UTC timestamp.

nodes.name: DispatchReceipt
nodes.summary: Inbound receipt model a specialist returns after a dispatch — correlation_id, outcome, output artifacts, diagnostics, and duration in milliseconds.

nodes.name: build_dispatch_envelope
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a DispatchEnvelope from dispatch kind, run id, input packet, and context refs, filling in correlation id and timestamp defaults.

nodes.name: build_dispatch_receipt
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a DispatchReceipt tying a specialist's outcome, artifacts, diagnostics, and duration back to the originating correlation id.

nodes.name: dials-spec.md
nodes.summary: Companion spec to the lesson-plan schema documenting the two operator-set dials (enrichment and corroboration), their 0.0-1.0 semantics, null defaults, dial interactions with the source-fitness signal, and canonical operator-facing wording.
nodes.languageNotes: Declares a split authority-of-record: schema.py wins on shape/validators, this doc wins on user-facing language.

nodes.name: html_review_pack.py
nodes.summary: Generates and manages static HTML review packs for gate reviews in a trial run: writes per-gate packs of slide rows with output digests, opens them in a browser with an open-log, archives prior versions, and blocks gate advance until the pack has been opened.

nodes.name: BrowserOpenError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when the review pack cannot be opened in the operator's browser.

nodes.name: GateAdvanceBlockedError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when a gate advance is attempted before the review pack open-log proves the pack was viewed.

nodes.name: ReviewPackRow
nodes.summary: Row model for one slide entry in a review pack (index, label, preview, output payload) with an output_digest method that sha256-hashes the canonical JSON of the output.

nodes.name: write_review_pack
nodes.summary: Writes the rendered HTML review pack for a trial/gate under the runs root, archiving any existing pack to a versioned history directory first.

nodes.name: open_review_pack
nodes.summary: Opens the review pack in the operator's browser via an injectable opener, raising BrowserOpenError on failure and recording a timestamped open-log used later to permit gate advance.

nodes.name: assert_gate_advance_allowed
nodes.summary: Guard that raises GateAdvanceBlockedError unless the gate's open-log exists and is non-empty, enforcing that the operator viewed the review pack before the gate advances.

nodes.name: _render_review_pack
nodes.summary: Renders the full self-contained HTML document for a gate review pack — header, styles, and one rendered row per slide — with all dynamic text HTML-escaped.

nodes.name: _render_row
nodes.summary: Renders a single slide row of the review pack including escaped preview/output content, verdict radio buttons, and the row's output digest.

nodes.name: learning_event_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Thin wiring layer that records Marcus gate decisions (Gate 2 approval, Gate 3 revision, Gate 4 waiver) as learning events appended to the run's ledger via the learning_event_capture utility.

nodes.name: record_gate_2_decision
nodes.summary: Creates a G2C approval learning event for the run and appends it to the run-directory ledger.

nodes.name: record_gate_3_decision
nodes.summary: Creates a G3 revision learning event for the run and appends it to the run-directory ledger.

nodes.name: record_gate_4_decision
nodes.summary: Creates a G4 waiver learning event for the run and appends it to the run-directory ledger.

nodes.name: stub_dials.py
nodes.summary: Read-only 'coming soon' dials affordance for the plan-drafting conversation surface (Story 30-3a): a frozen Pydantic model pinning Marcus's verbatim line, with no mutator API by contract until the tuning surface lands at 30-3b.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Literal-typed fields plus frozen ConfigDict to make the verbatim Marcus line and read-only mode unforgeable; a contract test asserts the absence of mutator methods.

nodes.name: StubDialsAffordance
nodes.summary: Frozen Pydantic model representing the read-only stub-dials placeholder: literal 'read-only' mode, the pinned Marcus line, and a frozenset of future dial names.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the Marcus pre-dispatch writer package (docstring only).

nodes.name: cp1252_fixture_compare.py
nodes.summary: CLI utility that compares two fixture files byte-wise after newline normalization, reporting equivalence, byte counts, the first divergence offset, and a cp1252-decoded context window as a JSON verdict.
nodes.languageNotes: Decodes divergence context with cp1252 and replacement errors so Windows-encoded fixture bytes render safely in diagnostics.

nodes.name: Cp1252FixtureComparisonVerdict
nodes.summary: Dataclass verdict of a fixture comparison: equivalence flag, byte counts for both sides, first divergence offset, and decoded divergence context.

nodes.name: compare_fixture_bytes
nodes.summary: Reads expected and actual fixture files, normalizes newlines, locates the first byte divergence, and returns a Cp1252FixtureComparisonVerdict with context around the divergence.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: argparse CLI entry point that runs compare_fixture_bytes on two paths and prints the verdict as JSON, returning a non-zero exit code on divergence.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the MCP transport bridge scaffolding package (docstring only).

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Minimal package README declaring app.mcp_server as MCP transport bridge scaffolding, justified by FR2, FR16, and FR35.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the MCP server tools subpackage (docstring only).

nodes.name: _frozen_hashes.py
nodes.summary: Exports FROZEN_AT_SHIP_HASHES, a dict of frozen pre-extension sha256 hashes for the g1/g2c/g3/g4 legacy DecisionCard shapes used by migration stories to detect schema drift.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Minimal package README declaring app.models.decision_cards as human-in-the-loop decision card model scaffolding, justified by FR31-FR33 and FR36.

nodes.name: party_mode_contribution.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: v1 JSON Schema for a PartyModeContribution — one persona's contribution to a consolidated party-mode review, with UUID contribution id, persona identity, structured payload, timezone-aware submission timestamp, and optional LangSmith trace link.

nodes.name: glossary_emit.py
nodes.summary: Generates a Markdown glossary from a YAML token registry: loads namespaces and token groups, renders them into dated glossary sections, and writes the output file, with a small CLI main entry.

nodes.name: render_glossary
nodes.summary: Renders the full glossary Markdown text from the parsed registry, iterating namespaces and token groups and stamping the generation date.

nodes.name: emit_glossary
nodes.summary: Loads the YAML token registry, renders the glossary Markdown, creates the output directory, and writes the glossary file.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that runs emit_glossary with the default registry and output paths.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Minimal package README declaring app.models.state as graph state model scaffolding, justified by FR38, FR46, FR47, and FR50.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Docstring-only package init documenting the NFR-M5 four-file-lockstep convention: every Pydantic state model ships a companion validator file here holding testable cross-field functions its model_validator delegates to (placeholder files for models without cross-field invariants).
nodes.languageNotes: Encodes a repo governance rule ('the validator file exists in the same PR as the model') rather than executable logic.

nodes.name: cache_state_validators.py
nodes.summary: NFR-M5 lockstep placeholder validator file for the CacheState model; ships only a docstring because CacheState has no cross-field invariants.

nodes.name: model_resolution_entry_validators.py
nodes.summary: NFR-M5 lockstep placeholder validator file for the ModelResolutionEntry model; docstring-only because the model's contract is fully field-level.

nodes.name: sanctum_fingerprint_validators.py
nodes.summary: NFR-M5 lockstep placeholder validator file for SanctumFingerprint, whose invariants (UUID4 identity, tz-aware timestamp, sha256 hex) are all expressible at the field layer, so no module-level functions ship.

nodes.name: specialist_envelope_validators.py
nodes.summary: NFR-M5 lockstep placeholder validator file for the SpecialistEnvelope model; docstring-only since the model has no cross-field invariants.

nodes.name: story_state_validators.py
nodes.summary: NFR-M5 lockstep placeholder validator file for the StoryState model; docstring-only since the model has no cross-field invariants.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Docstring-only package init for the validator companion package of top-level app.models schemas (PipelineRegistry, ModelSelectionPolicy), per the NFR-M5 four-file-lockstep convention.

nodes.name: registry_validators.py
nodes.summary: NFR-M5 lockstep placeholder for PipelineRegistry cross-field validators; the default-model-in-entries invariant currently lives inline on the model, with this file reserved as the extraction slot if the logic grows.

layers.name: Marcus Orchestration
layers.description: The Marcus-SPOC orchestrator, conversational CLI surface, lesson-plan compilation, course-source registry (canonical processed source, SME registry, syllabus metadata), and styleguide resolution wiring that drives an instance of the production runtime.

layers.name: Specialist Agents & Composers
layers.description: Per-specialist LangGraph agents (irene, gary, texas, enrique, vision, wanda, kira, motion_planner, quinn_r, and _shared audits) and the composer nodes — including the Section 02A directive composer — that assemble their outputs into decks, motion, and workbook artifacts.

layers.name: Gates & Workflow Substrate
layers.description: HIL gate decision modules (including the Section 02A G0 gate surface), the pipeline-manifest compiler, Cora block-mode dev-graph enforcement, and ledger/manifest substrate that gate and sequence the production walk.

layers.name: Domain Models
layers.description: Pydantic v2 domain and runtime-state models plus emitted JSON schemas under app/models that define decision-cards, lesson-plan envelopes, contributions side-cars, and the shared data contracts every specialist reads and writes.

layers.name: Runtime & Transport
layers.description: Runtime execution and persistence concerns: Postgres checkpointer/retention, resume-and-recover replay, the LiteLLM Batch transport package, HTTP transport, MCP server, parity checks, and audit plumbing that carry state through the graph.

layers.name: Operator & Developer Tooling
layers.description: Standalone scripts under scripts/ (BaseAPIClient and concrete API clients, content generators, analysis/acoustics diagnostics such as the source-fidelity report, operator utilities) used for live proofing and off-the-books discovery runs.

layers.name: BMAD Agent Skills
layers.description: Operator-facing BMAD agent skill packages under skills/ (SKILL.md persona files, reference contracts, and helper scripts) for the custom production personas Marcus routes work to.

layers.name: Project Root & Configuration
layers.description: Top-level project configuration and metadata: environment templates, git/coverage attributes, and analysis-scope tooling artifacts that sit at the repository root.

tour.title: Runtime Platform Overview
tour.description: Start with the package READMEs for the three pillars of the production runtime: app.models (the shared Pydantic model catalog every component reads and writes), app.manifest (the deterministic pipeline-manifest compiler), and app.runtime (LangGraph persistence — checkpointer, retention, thread cleanup). Together they frame the product goal: Marcus-SPOC, a conversational orchestrator that compiles a manifest into a LangGraph walk and drives specialist agents through human-in-the-loop gates. Keep this triangle — models, manifest, runtime — in mind; every later stop hangs off one of its corners.

tour.title: Marcus-SPOC CLI Entry
tour.description: The operator's front door is `python -m app.marcus.cli`, whose __main__.py builds the argparse tree aggregating trial (start/resume/recover/batch), gate, ad-hoc ask, plan-dialogue, and plan-ratify subcommands. marcus_spoc.py is the Marcus-SPOC conversational surface itself — the single point of contact that owns source material and learning objectives and drives an instance of the production runtime. This file is the highest fan-out node reachable from the operator: everything else in the tour is downstream of it.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `python -m package` convention executes a package's __main__.py, letting a package double as a CLI without installing console scripts. Argparse subparsers compose independent subcommand trees into one top-level command.

tour.title: Trial and Run Lifecycle
tour.description: trial.py wires the trial lifecycle commands the operator actually types: starting a run, resuming a paused one, and recovering from failures. A 'trial' is one tracked production run of a lesson through the pipeline, with per-run directories, receipts, and economics reporting. Understanding this lifecycle vocabulary — start, pause-at-gate, resume, recover — is essential before reading the runner it drives in the next step.

tour.title: Production Runner: Two Walks
tour.description: production_runner.py is the heart of the runtime and the highest fan-out file in the graph (43 imports): it compiles the manifest into a LangGraph and performs TWO node walks — a start walk that intentionally stops at gate G1, and a resume walk that continues after operator verdicts. Any gate-pause side-effect must land in BOTH walks, a subtle invariant that has bitten before. gate_runner.py is the companion that executes individual gate nodes and surfaces their decision cards to the operator.
tour.languageLesson: LangGraph models workflows as state graphs whose execution can be interrupted and resumed from a checkpoint. A 'walk' here is one invocation of the compiled graph; pausing at a human-in-the-loop gate means raising an interrupt and persisting state so a later walk can pick up exactly where the first stopped.

tour.title: Manifest Compiler
tour.description: The pipeline is not hand-wired: app/manifest is a tightly coupled cluster where schema.py defines the manifest shape, loader.py reads and validates the YAML pipeline manifest, and compiler.py deterministically compiles it into the node sequence the production runner walks. This manifest-compiled design is why the pipeline is auditable and why manifest edits are governance events, not casual code changes. The runner from Step 4 consumes this compiler's output directly.

tour.title: Gates, Verdicts, and the Ledger
tour.description: Human-in-the-loop gates pause the walk until the operator renders a verdict. resume_api.py (fan-in 28) is the shared surface gate modules use to accept verdicts and resume the graph, and operator_verdict.py is the Pydantic verdict model those surfaces validate against. Every gate event is also persisted to the append-only ledger_events table with idempotency keys — the durable audit trail of who approved what, when.
tour.languageLesson: Append-only event tables with idempotency keys are a classic audit pattern: instead of mutating a status column, every decision is a new immutable row, so replaying or double-submitting a verdict cannot corrupt history.

tour.title: Lesson Plan Model Layer
tour.description: Before any slides or narration exist, the lesson lives as a lesson plan: schema.py (fan-in 19) defines the plan's Pydantic model family, composition.py assembles plan units into a coherent whole, and the frozen lesson_plan.v1.schema.json is the emitted JSON Schema contract that pins the shape. This layer is where SOURCE material and learning objectives — the governing inputs — become typed, gateable plan structures the specialists downstream consume.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic v2 models can emit versioned JSON Schema files that are committed and frozen. Shape-pin tests then diff the emitted schema against the frozen file, turning accidental model changes into loud test failures instead of silent contract drift.

tour.title: Course-Source Registry
tour.description: New substrate: app/marcus/course_source is the registry that makes source material a first-class, validated citizen. canonical_processed_source.py enforces the ratified lesson-leaf layout, manifest_scan.py deterministically walks git-tracked corpus files and classifies them (with manifest_drift detection against stored snapshots), sme_registry.py resolves per-SME voice/styleguide/attribution, and syllabus_metadata.py extracts course metadata from DOCX/MHTML syllabi. The frozen canonical_asset_record schema is the evidence unit binding it all together — this is how 'SOURCE + LOs are KING' is enforced in code.

tour.title: Section 02A Composer and G0 Gate
tour.description: New substrate: the Section 02A composer is the first LLM-driven step that touches the corpus from Step 8 — composer.py walks a lesson corpus leaf, applies rule-based exclusions, and classifies each file's role into a typed directive (directive_model.py). The result is not trusted blindly: the Section 02A G0 poll_surface displays the directive with canonical SHA256 decision-card digests and lets the operator view, edit, and ratify it before the run proceeds. This pair is the canonical pattern for 'LLM proposes, operator disposes' at the very start of the walk.

tour.title: State Substrate and Specialist Contract
tour.description: Now the foundation everything above stands on: app/models/state. _base.py (fan-in 55, the most-depended-upon file in the repo) and run_state.py (fan-in 43) define the graph state that flows through every walk, while specialist_envelope.py and specialist_return.py define the typed payload a specialist receives and the typed result it must hand back. contract.py in _scaffold pins the contract every specialist implements — this uniformity is what lets the runner treat twenty different agents identically.

tour.title: Specialist LangGraph Roster
tour.description: Each specialist is its own LangGraph built on a standard 9-node scaffold, differing only in its act body. Irene authors the lesson narrative and collateral (her graph.py has the highest specialist fan-out at 17), Gary generates Gamma slide decks in classic and studio variants, Texas performs disciplined research retrieval, and Enrique produces directed ElevenLabs voice-over. Because all four honor the envelope/return contract from Step 10, Marcus dispatches to them uniformly — the roster grows by adding graphs, not by changing the runner.

tour.title: Runtime Persistence and Economics
tour.description: Two runtime concerns make long, gate-paused runs viable. checkpointer.py resolves Postgres connection settings and builds the LangGraph checkpointer, so a run paused at a gate on Tuesday can resume Thursday with full state intact (note: Postgres runs natively here — no Docker). economics.py (fan-in 16) tracks per-run cost accounting, feeding the trial economics reports the operator sees at run close.

tour.title: Batch LLM Execution Mode
tour.description: The newest runtime capability: an opt-in LiteLLM Batch transport (v1) that trades latency for roughly 50% provider cost on eligible call sites. llm_execution_config.py defines per-node realtime/batch transport profiles, llm_batch_eligibility.py loads the YAML matrix declaring which call sites may batch, and the llm_batch package does the lifecycle work — jsonl.py builds custom_id'd request rows, adapter.py drives the provider Files+Batches API, and receipts.py persists durable per-run BatchReceipts so an interrupted run can rejoin its in-flight batch. batch_route.py is the first consumer: 07G slide perception through the vision specialist.
tour.languageLesson: Provider Batch APIs work over JSONL: each line is a self-contained request tagged with a custom_id, and the async output file is joined back to callers by that id. Because completion can take hours, durable receipts plus a 'waiting' exception type let the run pause and later resume the join instead of blocking.

tour.title: Styleguide Resolution
tour.description: Visual identity is resolved, not hard-coded. resolver.py in app/styleguide is the CD-owned single source of truth for the Gamma styleguide library (re-homed from Gary's module, with a byte-compatible barrel left behind), and parity.py computes sha256-over-canonical-form hashes so any drift between resolution paths is detectable. Upstream, styleguide_picker.py serves a localhost card-grid page at CD-entry, letting the operator pick a style before the run — the choice then flows through resolution into Gary's deck generation from Step 11.
tour.languageLesson: The strangler-fig migration pattern: move a module to its new canonical home, then leave a byte-compatible re-export barrel at the old path so existing importers keep working while callers migrate incrementally.

tour.title: BMAD Agent Skills Layer
tour.description: Finally, the human-facing persona layer that sits beside the production runtime. skills/README.md indexes the custom production skills and BMAD personas, and Marcus's SKILL.md is the activation document an IDE agent reads to embody the orchestrator persona, with capabilities/registry.yaml declaring his typed capability schemas. Remember the guardrail: these persona-driven exploratory runs are a means of discovery — the production codebase you just toured, orchestrated by Marcus-SPOC, is the product.
